domain: law / common-law writs (habeas corpus / compulsory production)
specific_mechanism: Habeas corpus is a writ compelling a custodian to produce a detained person before a court and justify the detention; the burden is procedural (production + justification) and the remedy is release if the justification fails. The structural element is a forced-production protocol with bounded discretion: the custodian cannot refuse to produce; the court cannot refuse to inquire; the standard for justification is statutory not discretionary
llm_application: When a user queries an LLM agent 'why did you do X', invoke a habeas-corpus protocol: the runtime forces the agent to produce the intermediate reasoning trace; each step is procedurally validated against a statutory standard (was the predecessor step justified? was the input bounded?); if any step lacks procedural justification, the conclusion is released (the output is retracted with the failure point cited). The structural import is that production cannot be refused and justification follows a fixed legal-style standard, not the agent's discretion.
novelty_claim: Existing chain-of-thought audit and LLM-as-judge use voluntary inspection. No published 2024-2026 LLM/AI work formalizes a writ-of-habeas-corpus-style forced-production-with-statutory-justification protocol where the agent cannot refuse to produce its trace and the standard is exogenous, not discretionary.
